bullets: Gate 3 merged to master; forward-calibration join wired into --daily; funnel line (fetched->in-scope->routed->scored->cleared->fired) printed every cycle.
ntfy notifications (topic in atlas.yaml): every FIRE (high priority) + every daily-run failure (urgent; python-level via notify.py, shell-level via daily_run.sh curl).

Track D politics research engine v0.1 LIVE, scorecard-only: breadth pre-filter (all in-domain, <=14d, soonest-first), GDELT AP/Reuters dated headlines, LLM (claude CLI haiku) for synthesis ONLY — strict JSON factors/class/adjustment-categories; ALL probability math in code (coded base-rate menu, +/-.04/.08 adjustments, net cap .15, clamp [.05,.95]); conf hard 0.40 (cannot fire). Hard cap 40 LLM calls/run; per-run cost logged to data/research_costs.jsonl; month-to-date spend + scored/day + checkpoint ETAs on the scoreboard. Combat stays gated on Track C.
Economy: per the diagnostic, nothing moves; AAPL/NVDA watch-don't-trust.
changelog.title: v0.7 — 2026-07-18 (Gate 3 approved — full execution)

bullets: Two-tier wall filed in PROTOCOL.md (+ cherry-picked to master, docs only): Tier 1 sandbox — unlimited point-in-time quant experiments, logged in sandbox/LOG.md with mechanism-story-first entries and attempt counts; results are NEVER evidence, a win earns only a registration ticket. Tier 2 record — unchanged, the only truth.

Research-engine class excluded from sandbox entirely (hindsight contamination); accelerant = breadth: scorecard-only coverage widens to ALL in-domain pre-filter markets, ≤7d-resolution first; API cap respected; scored/day + projected checkpoint dates go in the weekly scoreboard (lands with Track D implementation post-gate).
Track D protocol drafts updated accordingly.
changelog.title: v0.6.1 — 2026-07-18 (SANDBOX TIER policy)

bullets: Track A': economy point-in-time diagnostic (SEC-by-filing-date + day- aligned Yahoo via as-of layer). n=10 (age-5) / 13 (age-1) — too thin; signal mildly AGAINST the model (market Brier better both variants); engine unchanged; AAPL/NVDA watch-don't-trust reinforced.

Track B: non-binary plumbing — named-binary markets (outcome-indexed ledger/costs/resolution, is_named_binary scope, fire-path skip). 12 new unit checks + live integration (506 named, 62 in scope, 0 scored).
Track D: research protocols v0.1 (politics, combat) + cost estimate (~$0-15/mo) + scorecard-only design. Never-backtest law for this class.
Standing asset: forward_calibration.py (scored-history outcome join + per-engine checkpoints). Wire-in awaits Gate 3.
changelog.title: v0.6 — 2026-07-18 (Phase 3 tracks — branch phase3-tracks, NOT merged)

bullets: Crypto v0.3 per registrations/2026-07-18_crypto_v0.3.md: per-kind Platt recalibration (touch A=-0.2088 B=0.6770; terminal A=-0.4468 B=1.1537, fit on 1,392 non-ETH backfill rows) + ETH-USD abstains (40% blind spot). Confidence tiers unchanged. Evidence: reports/backfill_crypto_v1.md.
Pre-registered holdout: non-ETH markets resolving after 2026-07-18 (+ live record); verdict at n>=300; PASS = beat market Brier AND beat v0.2-raw on same rows; FAIL is final for v0.3 (Family-1 rule).
Deploys to the daily loop from the 2026-07-19 run.
changelog.title: v0.5 — 2026-07-18 (crypto engine v0.3 — registered redesign)

engines.name: crypto
engines.note: v0.3: barrier/terminal price model on exchange daily closes, per-kind Platt recalibration frozen 2026-07-18. ETH-USD abstains — v0.2 was right on only 40% of ETH disagreements and the cause is unknown.

engines.name: economy
engines.note: v0.2: “largest company by market cap” races only, from SEC EDGAR shares + price history. All macro (Fed, CPI, recession) abstains — honest data paths not wired yet.

engines.name: sports
engines.note: v0.1 head-to-head form model on tiny free-data samples; confidence deliberately capped at 0.45, below the 0.60 firing bar. Tournament futures abstain. A real data feed is a paid decision, still open.

engines.name: combat
engines.note: No trustworthy free data source for MMA/boxing fundamentals; silence beats bad sources.

engines.name: politics
engines.note: Highest hindsight-leak domain — scan-time news is already priced in. A v0 model here would be vibes with citations.

stages.label: inside 20–80¢ band, live book
stages.why: price outside band, thin book, no two-sided quotes, non-Yes/No outcomes, or past scheduled end

stages.label: routed to an engine
stages.why: no domain match — unrouted markets are skipped by design

stages.label: scored (engine gave a probability)
stages.why: engine abstained: out of scope, ambiguous wording, or no trustworthy data source

stages.label: cleared the confidence bar
stages.why: engine confidence below the per-domain firing bar (crypto 0.65, economy 0.60)

stages.label: cleared the 2× cost hurdle
stages.why: edge vs the live book smaller than 2× modeled costs (fees + slippage + half-spread)

all.assumptions: In-sample evidence in live clothing: the v0.3 calibration was FIT on these rows, order books were not archived (2¢ spread assumed, base-band costs charged), and resolution timing is approximated by each market's horizon. The pre-registered holdout — not this replay — decides v0.3. Rules as deployed: ETH excluded, confidence ≥ 0.65, edge ≥ 2.0× costs, 1 pick per cluster, max 10 open, 1% compounding from $5,000.

crypto.label: crypto v0.3 rules replayed over the Jan 2025 – Jul 2026 harvest

economy.empty_reason: No replayable backtest: the only honest economy backfill (the cap-race diagnostic) is n=13 — far too small to simulate. This engine builds its record forward-only.
economy.label: economy engine

politics.empty_reason: Declared un-backtestable: news-driven markets can't be reconstructed as-of past dates without hindsight leaks. Forward-only by law.
politics.label: politics engine

research.empty_reason: Engine not built yet — nothing to replay.
research.label: research engine

sports.empty_reason: No backtest: free historical sports data is capped (TheSportsDB free tier) and a paid feed is an open decision. The engine scores live but its confidence is deliberately capped below the firing bar.
sports.label: sports engine

scored_table.question: Iran military action against a Gulf State on July 19?
scored_table.rationale: [research-politics-v0.1] class=status_quo_holds_1mo base=0.90 net_adj=+0.00 -> p=0.90. YES: Historical precedent of Iran-Gulf state tensions; Iran's demonstrated willingness to use proxies for military action | NO: No escalation signals in recent headlines; No incident reports in last 7 days; Status quo holding as of deadline | 0 headlines (AP/Reuters, 7d).

scored_table.question: Iran military action against a Gulf State on July 20?
scored_table.rationale: [research-politics-v0.1] class=status_quo_holds_1mo base=0.90 net_adj=-0.04 -> p=0.86. YES: Persistent Iran-Gulf military tensions as ongoing geopolitical backdrop; Iran's demonstrated willingness to conduct direct military operations (regional precedent) | NO: No escalatory headlines or rhetoric from AP/Reuters in past 7 days; Single-day resolution window with no reported military positioning or readiness alerts; Absence of diplomatic crisis or triggering incident in recent news | 0 headlines (AP/Reuters, 7d).

scored_table.question: Will Elon Musk post <40 tweets from July 18 to July 20, 2026?
scored_table.rationale: [research-politics-v0.1] class=status_quo_holds_1mo base=0.90 net_adj=-0.08 -> p=0.82. YES: Musk occasionally experiences periods of reduced posting during travel or major business events; 40 tweets across 3 days (≈13/day) is below his typical historical rate, suggesting constraint-heavy scenario; Summer season may include vacation or reduced operational intensity | NO: Musk's documented posting frequency typically ranges 20-50+ tweets per day under normal conditions; No reported travel, conference, or event conflicts mentioned for July 18-20; 3-day window is short; consistent with his baseline behavior pattern | 0 headlines (AP/Reuters, 7d).

scored_table.question: Will Elon Musk post 40-64 tweets from July 18 to July 20, 2026?
scored_table.rationale: [research-politics-v0.1] class=status_quo_holds_1mo base=0.90 net_adj=-0.04 -> p=0.86. YES: Elon Musk historically maintains extremely active tweeting, regularly posting 10-30+ times daily; 40-64 tweets over 3 days (13-21/day) falls within his documented baseline frequency range; X/Twitter is his primary communication channel; July 18-20 includes no known platform downtime or personal breaks | NO: Narrow 40-64 band is statistically difficult to hit; daily tweet volume exhibits high variance; Three-day observation windows show uneven distribution; missing target is common with tight precision bands; No recent news events to drive escalated or suppressed engagement above/below baseline | 0 headlines (AP/Reuters, 7d).

scored_table.question: Will Elon Musk post 140-159 tweets from July 14 to July 21, 2026?
scored_table.rationale: [research-politics-v0.1] class=status_quo_holds_1mo base=0.90 net_adj=-0.04 -> p=0.86. YES: Elon Musk maintains high baseline posting frequency on X/Twitter with multiple posts per day; Proposed target of ~17–20 tweets per day falls within historically observed posting patterns; No reported access restrictions or major events affecting platform engagement during this period | NO: Narrow band (140–159 tweets) creates specific targeting requirement; daily posting frequency fluctuates based on business developments and external events; Historical volatility in daily posting count makes precise ranges difficult to hit consistently; Imminent deadline with partial period already elapsed limits ability to adjust for actual observed behavior | 0 headlines (AP/Reuters, 7d).

scored_table.question: Will Elon Musk post 160-179 tweets from July 14 to July 21, 2026?
scored_table.rationale: [research-politics-v0.1] class=status_quo_holds_1mo base=0.90 net_adj=+0.00 -> p=0.90. YES: Elon Musk maintains active X presence as platform owner; Historical baseline suggests consistent high posting volume | NO: 160-179 tweets in 8 days requires ~20+ posts/day, above typical pace; No timeline-specific context provided | 0 headlines (AP/Reuters, 7d).

scored_table.question: Iran military action against a Gulf State on July 21?
scored_table.rationale: [research-politics-v0.1] class=status_quo_holds_1mo base=0.90 net_adj=+0.00 -> p=0.90. YES: Iran possesses demonstrated capability to conduct maritime and land-based strikes against Gulf targets; Persistent Iran-GCC tensions and ongoing regional proxy competition | NO: No reported escalatory signals or military mobilization in available data; Short 3-day window makes spontaneous unplanned escalation unlikely; Gulf states maintain active air defenses and regional deterrence posture | 0 headlines (AP/Reuters, 7d).

scored_table.question: Israel x Iran ceasefire continues through July 22?
scored_table.rationale: [research-politics-v0.1] class=status_quo_holds_1mo base=0.90 net_adj=+0.04 -> p=0.94. YES: 3-day window is too short for major escalation or formal breach; Ceasefire in place suggests active diplomatic channels; No reported provocations in recent week | NO: Israel-Iran confrontations historically volatile and prone to rapid escalation; Proxy forces (Hezbollah, PMU) can act independently of formal agreements; Accidental escalation or miscalculation risk during heightened tension | 0 headlines (AP/Reuters, 7d).

scored_table.question: Iran military action against a Gulf State on July 22?
scored_table.rationale: [research-politics-v0.1] class=status_quo_holds_1mo base=0.90 net_adj=+0.00 -> p=0.90. YES: Historical Iran-Gulf State military tensions; July 22 falls within ongoing regional instability period | NO: No escalatory military activity reported in last 7 days; No apparent trigger event announced for July 22 date | 0 headlines (AP/Reuters, 7d).

scored_table.question: Will Elon Musk post <40 tweets from July 20 to July 22, 2026?
scored_table.rationale: [research-politics-v0.1] class=status_quo_holds_1mo base=0.90 net_adj=+0.00 -> p=0.90. YES: Elon Musk's historical baseline tweeting volume typically exceeds 40 tweets across any 3-day period; No reported major disruptions or scheduling conflicts in the prediction window | NO: Possible reduced engagement during critical operational priorities or business crises | 0 headlines (AP/Reuters, 7d).

scored_table.question: Will Elon Musk post 40-64 tweets from July 20 to July 22, 2026?
scored_table.rationale: [research-politics-v0.1] class=status_quo_holds_1mo base=0.90 net_adj=+0.00 -> p=0.90. YES: Musk's documented history as a highly active Twitter user; 13-21 tweets/day over 3 days falls within his typical daily posting range; No reported major events constraining his availability during July 20-22 | NO: Absence of specific catalysts (product launches, controversies, earnings) that drive elevated posting; His daily posting volume is volatile and context-dependent; Business travel or intensive focus on X/Tesla operations could suppress activity | 0 headlines (AP/Reuters, 7d).

scored_table.question: Iran military action against a Gulf State on July 23?
scored_table.rationale: [research-politics-v0.1] class=status_quo_holds_1mo base=0.90 net_adj=+0.00 -> p=0.90. YES: Historical pattern of Iranian military posturing against Gulf states and strategic competitors; Ongoing regional tensions over Strait of Hormuz and sanctions regime; IRGC track record of conducting limited strikes or operations to signal resolve | NO: Absence of recent escalation headlines or elevated threat warnings in last 7 days; U.S. naval presence and air defense systems provide credible deterrence; Economic costs of direct military action given existing sanctions pressure | 0 headlines (AP/Reuters, 7d).

scored_table.question: Will Elon Musk post 160-179 tweets from July 17 to July 24, 2026?
scored_table.rationale: [research-politics-v0.1] class=status_quo_holds_1mo base=0.90 net_adj=+0.00 -> p=0.90. YES: Elon Musk maintains active, frequent posting patterns on X/Twitter; July 17-24 is a regular business period with no known major disruptions announced | NO: 160-179 tweets per week (~23-26 daily) requires sustained high-frequency engagement; No baseline data provided to confirm this rate matches his historical weekly average | 0 headlines (AP/Reuters, 7d).
